title: Watchdog LEVERAGE_BREACH rule is not barbell-aware (false HIGH every Stage-2 tick)
source: Launch-night watchdog arming (2026-06-21 R1). Dry-launch tick surfaced the rule firing on the healthy live book.
why: execution.watchdog.evaluate_rules LEVERAGE_BREACH compares the WHOLE-ACCOUNT gross_leverage (which INCLUDES the deliberate Stage-2 held-to-mark crypto lambda-bet, ~1.13 now rising toward ~2.0 at full basket build) against the SURVIVAL-BOOK-ONLY vol-target notional ceiling lev_ceiling=0.5 surfaced by webapp.state_reader (it reads diagnostics.leverage_gate.lev_ceiling). That is an apples-to-oranges comparison: the lambda-bet runs ABOVE the book's 0.5 ceiling by design, so the rule structurally false-fires (HIGH) on EVERY tick while Stage-2 is live. The genuine margin/leverage risk is already covered by MARGIN_LOW (margin_level) and the 30:1 account cap; lev_ceiling is a soft book vol-target, not a hard risk limit. On launch night the false positive was suppressed OPERATIONALLY (the rule was left in the monitor throttle 'firing' set so it never edge-triggers) and the monitor was armed at --min-severity HIGH; this ticket is the durable code fix.
value_rationale: Restores clean HIGH-severity watchdog coverage during the live window and every future Stage-2 session without the operational suppression hack. Read-only monitoring code — no capital/strategy impact, so safe to fix even under the freeze.
scope: TDD: add a failing test in tests/test_watchdog.py — a snapshot with stage2_active=True, stage2_lambda=1.5, lev_ceiling=0.5, whole-account gross_leverage in [1.1, 2.05] must NOT raise LEVERAGE_BREACH; a snapshot where the BOOK's own gated leverage exceeds the ceiling (real gate failure) MUST still raise.

Surface book-vs-total leverage cleanly: add additive keys to webapp.state_reader.read_snapshot risk block — at minimum stage2_active + stage2_lambda (already in diagnostics), and ideally book_gross_leverage (diagnostics.leverage_gate.gross_leverage_post). Additive only — must not change existing keys (the live dashboard reads this).

Fix the rule: either (a) compare the BOOK's gated leverage against lev_ceiling (semantically exact — detects a real gate failure, immune to the lambda-bet), or (b) compare whole-account gross against a barbell-aware ceiling = lev_ceiling + (stage2_lambda if stage2_active) with generous headroom for held-book drift above the vol-target. Pick the one that keeps a true runaway detectable.
Update tests/test_state_reader.py for the new snapshot keys. Full suite green.
Once merged: remove the operational note in the runbook about arming the watchdog at HIGH-with-suppression and confirm a clean HIGH dry-launch tick.
recommendation: Option (a) — compare the book's own gated leverage against lev_ceiling — is the most robust (no magic thresholds, immune to lambda level / basket fill state). Defer until not under launch-night pressure; the operational suppression + HIGH arming holds in the meantime.
out_of_scope: No change to the trading service, sizing, the leverage GATE itself, or any §14/§13 enforcement. Pure watchdog observability.
